누가복음 15:1.content: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.

누가복음 15:2.content: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”

누가복음 15:4.content: “Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?

누가복음 15:5.content: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

누가복음 15:6.content: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

누가복음 15:7.content: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

누가복음 15:8.content: Or what woman, if she had ten drachma coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?

누가복음 15:9.content: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.’

누가복음 15:10.content: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”

누가복음 15:11.content: He said, “A certain man had two sons.

누가복음 15:12.content: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ He divided his livelihood between them.

누가복음 15:13.content: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.

누가복음 15:14.content: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.

누가복음 15:15.content: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.

누가복음 15:16.content: He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.

누가복음 15:17.content: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!

누가복음 15:18.content: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.

누가복음 15:19.content: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.” ’

누가복음 15:20.content: “He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

누가복음 15:21.content: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’

누가복음 15:22.content: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and sandals on his feet.

누가복음 15:23.content: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let’s eat, and celebrate;

누가복음 15:24.content: for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ Then they began to celebrate.

누가복음 15:25.content: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

누가복음 15:26.content: He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.

누가복음 15:27.content: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

누가복음 15:28.content: But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.

누가복음 15:29.content: But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.

누가복음 15:30.content: But when this your son came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’

누가복음 15:31.content: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

누가복음 15:32.content: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ ”
